name: AI Tagger Universe

releases.author: Agents365-ai
releases.description:
## What's changed

- **Fix:** `generate-tags-for-current-note` now reads the predefined-tags file in Predefined Tags mode instead of only vault tags, so it no longer throws an error when the vault has no existing tags. (#63)
- **Fix:** Predefined Tags mode no longer applies invented tags or prose/echoed-prompt text as tags — matches are validated against the predefined list, and absurdly long fallback "tags" are dropped. This fixes the strange long tags some users saw with weaker models. (#61)
- **Feature:** Right-click a folder to batch-tag all notes inside it (including nested folders). (#62)
- **Fix:** Clearer Gemini connection-test message when the model name is wrong or retired (HTTP 404). (#57)

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/niehu2018/obsidian-ai-tagger-universe/compare/1.0.18...1.0.19
releases.name: v1.0.19

releases.author: Agents365-ai
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **#56 — YAML frontmatter case** (PR #55 by @knottybiscotti, plus follow-up): non-lowercase tag keys (`Tags:`, `TAGS:`, etc.) are now recognized everywhere, including the vault-wide tag scan. The original casing is preserved on write.
- **#59 — Tags written to wrong note**: the *Generate tags for current note* command now captures the target file at command time, so tags land on the note that was open when you ran the command — even if you switch notes while the LLM is still responding.
- **#57 — Misleading 404 error**: 404 from cloud connection tests now points at the model name as the likely cause (Gemini, OpenRouter etc. return 404 for unknown models, not bad URLs).
- **`getTagsFromFile` round-trip**: the tags file produced by the *Collect all tags* command (bare-word lines) again reads back correctly; markdown list items (`- tag`) also work.

## New

- **#58 — Excluded Tags setting** (Tagging settings): comma- or newline-separated list of tags to never apply, regardless of mode. Applied as a deterministic post-filter (LLMs are unreliable at "don't output X" prompt instructions). Matching is case-insensitive and ignores spaces / hyphens / underscores, so `Meeting Notes` blocks `meeting-notes`, `meeting_notes`, etc.

Thanks to @knottybiscotti, @cretep, @swimmingitmg, and @hokfujow for the reports.
releases.name: v1.0.18

releases.author: niehu2018
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes & Improvements
- **Configurable request timeout** — Default increased from 30s to 60s, now adjustable (15s–300s) in Settings → Tagging → Tag Behavior Settings (#54)
- **Preserve existing tags option** — New "Preserve Existing Tags" toggle in settings; when enabled, AI tags merge with existing ones instead of replacing (#50)
- **Improved LM Studio error messages** — HTTP 502/503 errors now show actionable troubleshooting hints (#53)

### Fixed
- Generate command now respects the replace/merge tags setting instead of always replacing
releases.name: v1.0.17

releases.author: niehu2018
releases.description:
## What's New in v1.0.16

### 🆕 Tag Management Features (New!)

- **Tag Analytics Dashboard** - View usage statistics, orphaned tags, and health metrics for your vault's tag system
- **Bulk Tag Rename** - Rename tags across your entire vault with one command
- **Tag Import/Export** - Backup and restore tags in CSV or JSON format
- **Tag Templates** - Define reusable tag sets for quick application to notes
- **Smart Tag Deduplication** - Find and merge similar tags (e.g., "ml" ↔ "machine-learning", "JavaScript" ↔ "javascript")

### New AI Providers
- **GLM (Zhipu AI)** - Native support for China's leading AI service
- **MiMo (Xiaomi)** - Xiaomi's MiMo-V2-Flash model
- **Minimax** - MiniMax-M2.1 model

### Tag Format Options
Choose your preferred naming convention for generated tags:
- kebab-case (my-tag-name)
- camelCase (myTagName)
- PascalCase (MyTagName)
- snake_case (my_tag_name)
- Original (preserve as-is)

### Enhanced Tag Network Visualization
- **Adjustable force settings** - Sliders for repulsion strength and link distance
- **Click-to-show documents** - Click any tag node to see all notes containing it
- **Real-time updates** - Network refreshes automatically when tags change
- **Manual refresh button**

### New Commands
- **Flatten hierarchical tags** - Convert nested tags (a/b/c) into separate flat tags (a, b, c)
  - Available for current note, folder, or entire vault

### Security & Code Quality Fixes
- Fixed XSS vulnerability in settings UI and tag network visualization
- Fixed memory leak in event handlers
- Added ReDoS protection for regex patterns
- Improved type safety across adapters
- Added proper cleanup for D3.js when multiple views are open

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed Claude API CORS compatibility for browser-based access

---
Full changelog: https://github.com/niehu2018/obsidian-ai-tagger-universe/compare/1.0.15...1.0.16
releases.name: v1.0.16

releases.author: niehu2018
releases.description:
## 🆕 New Features

### 🌳 Nested/Hierarchical Tags Support
- Generate hierarchical tags like `technology/artificial-intelligence/machine-learning`
- Configurable max nesting depth (1-3 levels)
- Smart LLM prompting for category-based organization
- Fully integrated with bilingual interface
- Backward compatible (disabled by default)

### 🎨 Custom Prompt UX Improvements
- Added helpful default prompt template for custom tagging mode
- Users can now modify the template instead of starting from scratch
- Improved descriptions and placeholder text for better discoverability

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fixed missing icons in ribbon and top-right toolbar (replaced invalid 'network' icon with 'git-graph')
- Fixed incorrect tool labels in Popular Tools Tips (OpenAI → Ollama for port 11434)
- Fixed custom prompt migration for existing users

## ♻️ Code Quality

- Removed redundant "Assign Predefined tags" commands (use main "Generate tags" commands with "Use predefined tags only" mode instead)
- Improved settings organization (moved Support section to bottom)
- Added comprehensive CLAUDE.md documentation for future development

## 🧪 Testing

- Added automated testing suite with 32 validation tests (100% pass rate)
- Enhanced tag formatting to preserve forward slashes in nested tags

## 📝 Documentation

- Updated README files (English and Chinese) for v1.0.15
- Better documentation for custom prompt feature
- Added nested tags usage examples

## 🔗 Closed Issues

- #34: Bug report: Missing icons in ribbon and top-right toolbar
- #26: Assign Predefined tags for Current Note - "Assign Failed"
- #20: Support for nested tags
- #12: Feature Request: Chinese Translation

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/niehu2018/obsidian-ai-tagger-universe/compare/1.0.14...1.0.15
releases.name: v1.0.15 - Nested Tags & UX Improvements

releases.author: niehu2018
releases.description:
### Highlights
- Prevents prefixed tag values like `matchedExistingTags-supply-chain` and `suggestedTags-distribution` at the source via stricter prompts
- Adds defensive parsing to sanitize any stray tag prefixes before saving

### Changes
- Prompts: clarify hybrid JSON format and forbid key names inside tag values
- All modes: explicitly forbid label/prefixes in tag values, provide clean examples
- System prompt: follow requested output format (JSON vs list), no # in tag values, no prefixes
- Parser: sanitize values to strip leading `matchedExistingTags-`, `suggestedTags-`, etc.

### Notes
- If you see unexpected tag formatting from some models, the parser now normalizes most cases automatically.
- Hybrid mode returns clean JSON with separate `matchedExistingTags` and `suggestedTags` arrays.
releases.name: v1.0.14

releases.author: niehu2018
releases.description:
This release (version 1.0.13) focuses on simplifying the local LLM settings interface to enhance usability. Key changes include:

1. Removed the "Local Service Type" dropdown, making configuration more straightforward
2. Replaced complex model selection with a simple text input for model name
3. Set Ollama endpoint as the default local LLM endpoint (http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions)
4. Added a helpful "Tips" section listing popular local LLM tools with their default endpoints:
   - Ollama: http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions
   - LM Studio: http://localhost:1234/v1/chat/completions
   - Jan: http://localhost:1337/v1/chat/completions
   - LocalAI: http://localhost:8080/v1/chat/completions

These changes significantly reduce complexity while maintaining full functionality, making it easier for users to configure and use local language models with the plugin.

releases.author: niehu2018
releases.description:
# AI Tagger Universe v1.0.12

## 🚀 New Features and Improvements

### Enhanced Local LLM Service Integration
- Improved handling of local LLM services (Ollama, LM Studio, LocalAI)
- Added service availability checks when switching between local services
- Prevent model crossover between different services (e.g., Ollama models won't appear in LM Studio dropdown)
- Added clear user notifications when a local service is not running

### Better Error Handling and User Feedback
- Enhanced error messages with service-specific guidance
- Added helpful prompts for downloading models when none are found
- Improved model fetching with service-specific error handling
- Clear instructions for setting up each local service type

### UI/UX Improvements
- Cleaner model selection dropdown that only shows relevant models for the current service
- Better status messages when services are unavailable
- Updated styles for better visual consistency

### Other Changes
- Added `.roo/` to `.gitignore`
- Various code optimizations and cleanup
- Updated tag utilities

## 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Fixed issue where models from one service would incorrectly show up in another service's dropdown
- Resolved model loading issues when switching between different local services
- Improved error handling during model fetching operations

## 📝 Notes
- When switching to a local service (Ollama, LM Studio, or LocalAI), the system will now check if the service is running
- If a service is not running, you'll receive a clear notification about what needs to be done
- Model lists are now specific to each service, providing a cleaner and more accurate interface

releases.author: niehu2018
releases.description:
We're excited to announce AI Tagger Universe v1.0.7, a major update that introduces powerful new tag management features and visualization capabilities to help you organize your Obsidian vault more effectively.

What's New
🔍 Tag Network Visualization
Interactive network graph showing relationships between tags
Visual representation of tag frequency and connections
Search functionality to find specific tags
Hover tooltips with detailed tag information
Zoom and pan controls for exploring complex tag networks
🏷️ Enhanced Tag Management
New batch tagging operations for multiple notes
File context menu integration for quick tagging
Folder-level operations for managing tags in specific directories
Improved tag clearing functionality with better frontmatter preservation
🤖 Multi-Provider AI Support
Added support for multiple new AI providers:
Groq LLM
Grok
Mistral AI
DeepSeek
Aliyun Qwen
And more!
🔧 Improved Settings & Configuration
Dedicated settings tab with improved organization
More granular control over tag generation parameters
Support for predefined tag libraries
Better error handling and user feedback

🧩 Other Improvements
Comprehensive command palette integration
Ribbon icons for quick access to common operations
Performance optimizations for large vaults
Improved error handling and user notifications
Under the Hood
Complete code refactoring for better maintainability
New adapter-based architecture for AI service integration
Enhanced prompting system for more accurate tag generation
Better frontmatter handling and preservation
This release represents a significant step forward in making AI-powered tagging more accessible, flexible, and powerful for Obsidian users. The new visualization tools help you understand the relationships between your tags, while the enhanced management features make it easier to organize your knowledge base.

Thank you for using AI Tagger Universe!
releases.name: AI Tagger Universe v1.0.7: Enhanced Tag Management & Visualization

releases.author: niehu2018
releases.description:
# AI Tagger Universe v1.0.1

AI Tagger Universe is an intelligent tagging assistant that automatically analyzes your notes and suggests relevant tags, leveraging both local and cloud AI services.

## Features

- **Multiple AI Service Support:**
  - Local LLM via Ollama (default)
  - Cloud LLM services (OpenAI, Azure)
  - Custom API endpoints
  - Configurable model selection

- **Smart Tag Analysis:**
  - Matches 1-3 tags from existing tag library
  - Generates 3-10 new relevant tags
  - Automatic frontmatter integration

- **Flexible Configuration:**
  - Choose between local or cloud services
  - Adjust tag generation parameters
  - Customize API endpoints and models

## Requirements

- Obsidian v1.4.0 or higher
- For local LLM: Ollama installed locally
- For cloud services: Valid API key

## Installation

1. Open Obsidian Settings
2. Go to Community Plugins
3. Search for "AI Tagger Universe"
4. Click Install, then Enable

## Quick Start

1. Configure your preferred AI service in plugin settings
2. Open a note
3. Use command palette (Cmd/Ctrl + P)
4. Search for "Analyze Current Note and Add Tags"

## Keyboard Shortcuts

- `Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + T`: Clear all tags while keeping the tags field

## Support

If you find this plugin helpful, consider supporting the development:
- Buy Me a Coffee: https://buymeacoffee.com/niehu2015o
- GitHub: https://github.com/niehu2018/obsidian-ai-tagger-universe

## Changes

First release with:
- Dual AI service support (local/cloud)
- Smart tag analysis and generation
- Comprehensive settings interface
- Tag management features
